sub_fields.label: Header Paragraph
sub_fields.name: header_tagline
sub_fields.default_value: Personalized acupuncture and dry needling care to help your body heal, naturally.
sub_fields.placeholder: Enter a short introduction or tagline

fields.label: About Section Title
fields.name: about_title
fields.default_value: About Alison — Your Cobourg Acupuncturist & RPN

fields.label: About Body Text
fields.name: about_text
fields.default_value: Alison's mission is to help every patient move, sleep, and live with less pain — using the time-tested principles of Traditional Chinese Medicine alongside the clinical expertise of a Registered Practical Nurse. Each treatment plan is built around you: your symptoms, your goals, and your body's own capacity to heal. Whether you're managing chronic pain, recovering from injury, or simply seeking better balance, Alison combines acupuncture, dry needling, and hands-on techniques in a calm, welcoming space in Cobourg — because healing works best when you feel cared for, not just treated.

sub_fields.label: Acupuncture Title
sub_fields.name: acupuncture_title
sub_fields.default_value: Acupuncture

sub_fields.label: Acupuncture Description
sub_fields.name: acupuncture_description
sub_fields.default_value: Acupuncture is a Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) practice where thin, solid needles are inserted into specific strategic points in the skin. It aims to correct imbalances in the body's flow of vital energy (Qi) and stimulate the central nervous system to promote natural healing and pain relief.

sub_fields.label: Electroacupuncture Title
sub_fields.name: electroacupuncture_title
sub_fields.default_value: Electroacupuncture

sub_fields.label: Electroacupuncture Description
sub_fields.name: electroacupuncture_description
sub_fields.default_value: Electroacupuncture is a modified form of traditional acupuncture where a mild electrical current passes between pairs of acupuncture needles. This technique combines ancient holistic practices with modern technology to deliver continuous, controlled stimulation to targeted body tissues. It is widely used by clinical practitioners to manage acute or chronic pain, reduce inflammation, and enhance overall healing.

sub_fields.label: Dry Needling Title
sub_fields.name: dryneedling_title
sub_fields.default_value: Dry Needling

sub_fields.label: Dry Needling Description
sub_fields.name: dryneedling_description
sub_fields.default_value: Dry needling is a modern, evidence-based medical treatment where a clinician inserts thin, solid filiform needles directly into myofascial trigger points (painful muscle knots). The term "dry" means the procedure does not inject any medication or fluid into the body. Instead, it relies entirely on the physical interaction between the needle and the muscle tissue to deactivate pain triggers and restore normal movement.

sub_fields.label: Cupping Title
sub_fields.name: cupping_title
sub_fields.default_value: Cupping

sub_fields.label: Cupping Description
sub_fields.name: cupping_description
sub_fields.default_value: Cupping therapy is an ancient alternative medicine practice where a clinician places specialized cups on the skin for a few minutes to create suction. This negative pressure pulls the skin, superficial muscle layer, and fascial tissue upward into the cup. The primary goal of the treatment is to increase blood flow, encourage cell repair, relieve muscle tension, and accelerate overall tissue healing.

sub_fields.label: Tissue Work Title
sub_fields.name: tissuework_title
sub_fields.default_value: Soft Tissue Therapy

sub_fields.label: Tissue Work Description
sub_fields.name: tissuework_description
sub_fields.default_value: Soft tissue therapy involves manipulating muscles, tendons, ligaments, and fascia to alleviate tension, break down scar tissue, and improve blood flow. It aids recovery, increases flexibility, and relieves chronic pain through techniques like deep tissue massage, myofascial release, and foam rolling

sub_fields.label: Lower Body Tissue Work Title
sub_fields.name: lowertissuework_title
sub_fields.default_value: Lower Body Tissue Work

sub_fields.label: Lower Body Tissue Work Description
sub_fields.name: lowertissuework_description
sub_fields.default_value: Foot and leg tissue work involves therapeutic techniques—such as deep tissue massage, myofascial release, and trigger point therapy—that target the muscles, tendons, and fascia of the lower limbs. It aims to relieve tension, improve circulation, enhance joint mobility, and accelerate recovery from injuries or overuse.

sub_fields.label: Gua Sha Title
sub_fields.name: guasha_title
sub_fields.default_value: Gua Sha

sub_fields.label: Gua Sha Description
sub_fields.name: guasha_description
sub_fields.default_value: Gua Sha is a traditional East Asian healing technique where a practitioner uses a smooth-edged tool to scrape the skin with light-to-medium pressure. The term translates directly from Chinese: "Gua" means to scrape or rub, and "Sha" refers to the reddish, sand-like petechiae (tiny red spots) that surface on the skin during treatment. The technique breaks up tissue stagnation, releases tight fascia, and rapidly increases localized blood flow to promote healing.

sub_fields.label: Ear Wax Flushing Title
sub_fields.name: earwax_title
sub_fields.default_value: Ear Irrigation

sub_fields.label: Ear Wax Flushing Description
sub_fields.name: earwax_description
sub_fields.default_value: Ear Irrigation, is a routine medical procedure used to remove a buildup of impacted cerumen (ear wax) from the ear canal. The process uses a controlled stream of warm water or saline to gently flush the wax out. It is commonly performed by healthcare professionals when excess ear wax causes symptoms like muffled hearing, earaches, or a feeling of fullness.

sub_fields.label: Balance Support Care Title
sub_fields.name: balancecare_title
sub_fields.default_value: Dizziness and Balance Support Care

sub_fields.label: Balance Support Care Description
sub_fields.name: balancecare_description
sub_fields.default_value: Dizziness and Balance Support Care - often clinically referred to as Vestibular Rehabilitation Therapy (VRT)—is a specialized, exercise-based medical program designed to treat balance disorders, chronic dizziness, and spatial disorientation. The care is primary coordinated by specialized physical therapists or audiologists. The central goal is to retrain the brain to process signals from the balance systems correctly, reduce fall risks, and restore daily confidence

sub_fields.label: Medication Education Title
sub_fields.name: medication_education_title
sub_fields.default_value: Medication Education

sub_fields.label: Medication Education Description
sub_fields.name: medication_education_description
sub_fields.default_value: Medication Education is a personalized clinical service provided by pharmacists, nurses, or physicians to teach patients how to safely, accurately, and effectively take their prescribed medications. This educational process ensures you understand exactly why you are taking a drug, how to take it correctly, and what to expect regarding side effects. The ultimate goal is to prevent dangerous medical errors, avoid adverse drug interactions, and improve health outcomes.

fields.label: Blood Pressure and Heart Rate Monitoring
fields.name: heart_health

sub_fields.label: Blood Pressure and Heart Rate Monitoring Title
sub_fields.name: heart_health_title
sub_fields.default_value: Blood Pressure and Heart Rate Monitoring

sub_fields.label: Blood Pressure and Heart Rate Monitoring Description
sub_fields.name: heart_health_description
sub_fields.default_value: Blood Pressure and Heart Rate Monitoring is a vital healthcare practice used to track, record, and analyze your cardiovascular health over time. Blood pressure measures the force of blood pushing against your artery walls, while heart rate (pulse) counts the number of times your heart beats per minute. Regular monitoring helps doctors catch early signs of cardiovascular disease, evaluate how well your heart medications are working, and prevent serious complications like heart attacks or strokes.
